noteP1: We picked fifteen companies from our portfolio that map to the priorities of Chiquita's information and technology organization: frontier AI and the infrastructure to run it economically, the data foundations underneath, and the security and developer tooling that let both move to production.
noteP2: Each card opens to a brief and the angle we think fits Chiquita best, and you can filter by category. Our full portfolio of 150+ active companies is linked in the top right. Whenever it's convenient, send back the ones worth a closer look.
portfolioIntro: Every company here works at the infrastructure layer, across AI, data, security, and developer tooling, with an angle on where it fits Chiquita's agenda. Filter by category, then expand any card for the brief.

anthropic.pull: Chiquita is modernizing a global perishable-produce operation where farm, packing, shipping, cold-chain and retail data determine quality, resilience and sustainability. The hardest work is often buried in farm records, crop conditions, lot identifiers, quality inspections, shipping events, temperatures and sustainability measures and the systems that surround them. Anthropic matters because Claude can handle long, interdependent context, support agentic workflows and give developers Claude Code inside the same enterprise AI strategy. That creates a practical bridge between growers, agronomists, plant teams, logistics planners, quality staff and commercial teams and the teams maintaining farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications. Deployed with explicit human checkpoints and evaluation, it can advance better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance while keeping food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations visible rather than implicit.
cases: Reason across farm records, crop conditions, lot identifiers, quality inspections, shipping events, temperatures and sustainability measures without forcing teams into a separate knowledge system.
Use Claude Code across farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications and the integration services behind them.
Keep human review, model policy and audit evidence explicit in high-consequence workflows.

wispr.pull: The information gap at Chiquita is not always a missing database; often it is what growers, agronomists, plant teams, logistics planners, quality staff and commercial teams know but do not record during field observations, quality notes, maintenance reports, driver updates and customer calls. Wispr Flow lets users speak naturally into existing desktop and mobile tools, producing structured, edited text with commands and filler-word cleanup. That makes it well suited to distributed or frontline work around planting-to-pack, cold-chain and farm-to-retail workflows. The information and technology organization gains more timely operational context without asking teams to adopt a separate note-taking system or spend the end of a shift reconstructing what happened.
cases: Turn field observations, quality notes, maintenance reports, driver updates and customer calls into structured entries while the context is still fresh.
Work inside farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications rather than creating another application to manage.
Create a richer decision trail for handoffs, analytics and supervised agents.

browserbase.pull: Chiquita is a vertically connected banana supply chain exposed to weather, disease, logistics disruption and strict food-safety requirements, but many critical interactions still happen through web interfaces rather than clean service integrations. Browserbase gives the technology team an infrastructure layer for agents to use carrier portals, customs sites, supplier systems, retailer tools and legacy operational web applications with controlled sessions and repeatable execution. It can also parallelize browser testing for farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications, catching failures before they reach growers, agronomists, plant teams, logistics planners, quality staff and commercial teams. That makes it a useful bridge between near-term automation and longer-term modernization by removing small web-based bottlenecks that stand in the way of better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance.
cases: Automate bounded tasks across carrier portals, customs sites, supplier systems, retailer tools and legacy operational web applications without waiting for every system to expose an API.
Run repeatable browser tests across farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications before customer- or employee-facing releases.
Centralize sessions, credentials and execution logs for governed agent activity.

coder.pull: Developer productivity at Chiquita is constrained when setup, hardware and local configuration differ across the teams building farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications. Coder replaces that variability with centrally configured cloud development environments that can be provisioned from policy and shut down when not in use. The model is relevant to food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations because code, credentials and regulated tools remain inside the enterprise boundary. It also gives new engineers and acquired or distributed teams a consistent starting point, shortening the path from access request to a productive, compliant workspace.
cases: Standardize cloud workspaces for teams building farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications.
Keep source code, credentials and approved AI tooling inside controlled infrastructure.
Reduce onboarding and environment drift across distributed, acquired or specialized teams.

token.pull: For the information and technology organization, AI-agent governance becomes an identity problem before it becomes a model problem. Chiquita is a vertically connected banana supply chain exposed to weather, disease, logistics disruption and strict food-safety requirements; adding device identities, integration accounts, cold-chain sensors, bots and operational agents increases the number of principals acting at machine speed. Token Security provides the discovery, ownership, least-privilege and audit layer required to keep that population controlled. Applied across hybrid cloud, field, plant and logistics workloads across countries and intermittent connections, it can show where credentials are over-permissioned or orphaned and make access changes traceable. That gives Chiquita a controlled path toward better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance and avoids an invisible tier of privileged automation.
cases: Inventory device identities, integration accounts, cold-chain sensors, bots and operational agents and tie every credential to an accountable owner.
Constrain agent access to the specific farm, crop, lot, quality, logistics, cold-chain and sustainability data required for each workflow.
Preserve lifecycle and entitlement evidence for audits, regulators and internal risk review.

fal.pull: Chiquita has practical multimodal needs across crop imagery, packhouse inspection video, training content and sustainability evidence. fal matters because it gives product and engineering teams a high-speed inference layer for image, video, audio and voice models through simple APIs, without forcing each use case to become an infrastructure project. The information and technology organization could use it to prototype and scale bounded generative experiences alongside farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications, from content production to simulation or visual assistance. The strongest fit is where latency and iteration speed matter but the risk profile around food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations still requires approved models, controlled inputs and a clear review path.
cases: Embed image, video, audio or voice generation into new farm, quality, traceability, logistics and sustainability capabilities.
Prototype against multiple media models through one developer-facing inference layer.
Keep review, consent and brand or safety controls in the surrounding application workflow.

montecarlo.pull: Chiquita's AI and operating decisions increasingly depend on farm, crop, lot, quality, logistics, cold-chain and sustainability data. A broken pipeline, silent schema change or weak model input can therefore surface as a service, safety or operational problem rather than a data-team ticket. Monte Carlo matters because it unifies observability across data pipelines, systems, code, models and the agents built on top. The information and technology organization would gain a common view of lineage, incidents and downstream impact across crop monitoring, quality prediction, logistics planning, maintenance and traceability agents. That trust layer is central to better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance, especially where early detection and clear ownership are essential to managing food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations.
cases: Monitor farm, crop, lot, quality, logistics, cold-chain and sustainability data from source through dashboards, models and agent actions.
Trace incidents to the affected farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications and accountable engineering teams.
Prevent silent data failures from undermining better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance.

tonic.pull: The teams building farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications need realistic data, but using production copies of worker, farm, crop, supplier, lot and shipment data creates unnecessary exposure and slows access approvals. Tonic matters because it can generate synthetic, de-identified datasets that preserve the shape and utility of enterprise data for development, testing and AI work. For Chiquita, that would let engineers reproduce edge cases, train or evaluate models and refresh lower environments without moving identifiable records into places they do not belong. The result is faster delivery with fewer sensitive records outside production—important in an environment shaped by food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations.
cases: Replace production copies of worker, farm, crop, supplier, lot and shipment data in development and test environments.
Generate rare and edge-case scenarios for farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications and AI evaluation.
Reduce privacy exposure while giving teams faster access to useful data.

netbox.pull: Chiquita is a vertically connected banana supply chain exposed to weather, disease, logistics disruption and strict food-safety requirements. That scale creates a persistent infrastructure-data problem: asset records, topology and configuration intent are often split across tools and local teams. NetBox Labs can centralize that context for farms, packhouses, ports, distribution sites, cloud services and field networks and expose it programmatically to operations and automation. That is useful for everyday network changes, but also for the agentic future—the safest infrastructure agent is one grounded in an authoritative model of devices, sites, addresses and dependencies. For Chiquita, that supports faster change with less operational ambiguity.
cases: Create a maintained source of truth across farms, packhouses, ports, distribution sites, cloud services and field networks.
Drive provisioning and change validation for hybrid cloud, field, plant and logistics workloads across countries and intermittent connections from intended state.
Ground infrastructure automation and agents in accurate topology and ownership data.

valar.pull: For Chiquita, the highest-value AI workloads sit close to farm, crop, lot, quality, logistics, cold-chain and sustainability data and often need bursty batch or agentic execution. Valar provides an in-VPC inference platform built for that pattern, using scheduling, routing and accelerator placement to turn installed compute into more usable capacity. It would give platform teams one operating layer for crop monitoring, quality prediction, logistics planning, maintenance and traceability agents, while prompts, outputs and telemetry remain within the enterprise boundary. That makes Valar a strong fit where external model traffic is constrained by food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations, but the business still needs production-grade throughput.
cases: Run crop monitoring, quality prediction, logistics planning, maintenance and traceability agents inside Chiquita's own VPC and security boundary.
Increase usable accelerator capacity through scheduling, batching and routing.
Keep prompts, outputs, logs, models and policies under enterprise control.

echo.pull: Chiquita's farm, quality, traceability, logistics and planning applications are assembled from thousands of base images, libraries and packages that can introduce vulnerabilities before application code is written. Echo matters because it supplies trusted, continuously patched and hardened software dependencies, including FIPS-validated options, at the component layer. The information and technology organization could use that foundation to reduce the vulnerability backlog across farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications and give developers secure defaults without slowing delivery. The fit is especially strong where the risk profile around food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations makes end-of-pipeline remediation too late or too expensive.
cases: Standardize hardened dependencies across farm, quality, traceability, logistics and planning applications.
Remove known vulnerabilities before they enter application and agent build pipelines.
Use FIPS-validated options where stronger assurance is required.

act.pull: Least privilege at Chiquita is increasingly a service-to-service problem. Chiquita's device identities, integration accounts, cold-chain sensors, bots and operational agents can reach across hybrid cloud, field, plant and logistics workloads across countries and intermittent connections, while ownership and network policy are split among different tools. Act Security provides a shared view of what can talk to what, what actually does and what the business intended. It can then help teams segment farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications around real application context. For the information and technology organization, the value is a practical route to smaller cloud blast radii without introducing agents or waiting for a full architecture rewrite.
cases: Map intended and actual communication across hybrid cloud, field, plant and logistics workloads across countries and intermittent connections.
Create least-privilege perimeters around farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications and their data services.
Reduce lateral-movement risk without deploying another host agent.

clover.pull: For Chiquita, shipping faster only works if security can understand the roadmap at the same speed. Clover Security integrates into the design and planning stage, where growers, agronomists, plant teams, logistics planners, quality staff and commercial teams are defining new farm, quality, traceability, logistics and sustainability capabilities. It can surface risks from the context already captured in tickets and documents, then return specific requirements before architecture and code harden around a weak assumption. The fit is strongest for farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications, where early decisions touch food safety, traceability, worker privacy, climate exposure and cross-border operations and where a preventable redesign near release would slow the broader technology program.
cases: Review new farm, quality, traceability, logistics and sustainability capabilities while requirements and architecture are still being shaped.
Map security decisions back to tickets, design documents and accountable owners.
Reduce late-stage rework across farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications without adding another approval queue.

drata.pull: Chiquita has to prove trust across food safety, labor, sustainability reporting, customs, privacy and third-party assurance while its technology estate and third-party relationships continue to change. Drata matters because it turns controls, evidence, risks and assurance signals into a continuously monitored trust graph rather than a periodic collection exercise. The information and technology organization could use it to connect policies and owners to the systems behind farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications, surface control drift and reuse evidence across overlapping reviews. That makes compliance more operational and gives leadership a current view of the risks that could slow progress toward better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance.
cases: Continuously map controls and evidence across farm, quality, traceability, logistics, planning, maintenance and commercial applications and hybrid cloud, field, plant and logistics workloads across countries and intermittent connections.
Reuse assurance work across food safety, labor, sustainability reporting, customs, privacy and third-party assurance instead of rebuilding it for every review.
Give leaders a current view of control drift, ownership and third-party risk.

orca.pull: Cloud and AI assets at Chiquita will change faster as the organization pursues its current agenda: modernizing a global perishable-produce operation where farm, packing, shipping, cold-chain and retail data determine quality, resilience and sustainability. Orca Security discovers that estate agentlessly, maps the relationships among workloads, identities, data and vulnerabilities, and ranks risk by likely impact. Applied to hybrid cloud, field, plant and logistics workloads across countries and intermittent connections, it can give security and platform teams a shared picture of the exposures affecting farm, crop, lot, quality, logistics, cold-chain and sustainability data. The result is fewer blind spots, less alert fatigue and a remediation plan grounded in actual attack paths—all in service of better quality and supply resilience from farm to retailer with verifiable provenance.
cases: Gain agentless visibility across Chiquita's cloud estate and emerging AI workloads.
Prioritize attack paths into sensitive Chiquita data and critical applications.
Unify posture, workload, data, vulnerability and compliance remediation in one view.
